title: Backyard Home (ADU) Feasibility Reports
oneLiner: Homeowners learn in minutes whether their lot can legally and physically hold a rental unit — before paying an architect.

tags.target: Homeowners in ADU-friendly states (CA, WA, OR, CO...)

summary.problem: ADU rules vary by city, lot, zoning overlay, setbacks, and utility access. Homeowners can't tell if their backyard qualifies without hiring professionals, so most never start — and the ones who do often pay for feasibility work that returns a 'no'.
summary.solution: Address-in, report-out: parcel data + local zoning rules + setback/slope analysis produce a feasibility report with buildable envelope, unit size allowed, estimated cost range, and rental income projection. Sell the report, then refer qualified leads to local ADU builders.
summary.wedge: One metro with clean parcel data and strong ADU demand; the report is the lead magnet for builder referral fees.

sources.title: IdeaBrowser — Idea of the Day email, 2026-06-10 (Backyard home reports)
